DispatchCondition.Title: Trailing Edge Variable Camber (Cruise Flaps) Operative

ListItem.Para: Center hydraulic system electric motor-driven pumps operate normally.
Trailing edge variable camber (cruise flaps) function operates normally.
Main landing gear priority valve is verified to operate normally once each flight day.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify main landing gear priority valve operates normally once each flight day (AMM DDG 27-81-01).

ListItem.Para: With engines not operating, pressurize the center hydraulic system using the operational center system pump.
Verify that the main landing gear pressure shown on the landing gear maintenance page is more than 4500 psi.
Turn off the operating center system pump.
Wait at least 10 seconds.
Verify that the main landing gear pressure shown on the landing gear maintenance page is more than 2000 psi.

DispatchCondition.Title: Trailing Edge Variable Camber (Cruise Flaps) Inoperative

ListItem.Para: Center hydraulic system electric motor-driven pumps operate normally.
Flap secondary mode is verified to operate normally once each flight day.
Main landing gear priority valve is verified to operate normally once each flight day.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify flap secondary mode and main landing gear priority valve operate normally once each flight day (AMM DDG 27-81-01).

ListItem.Para: Do the ground test of the flap secondary mode.
Confirm flaps are UP.
For all three hydraulic systems available, turn hydraulics on.
For only one or two of the hydraulic systems available at once:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever UP.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are extended.
Conduct the ground system test Flaps (Electric).

Note.Para: If hydraulic systems are pressurized, spoilers, ailerons, and flaperons will move during test.

ListItem.Para: After completion of the test, confirm the FLAPS SECONDARY FAIL status message is not displayed.
Retract spoilers if extended:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever DN.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are retracted.
Verify main landing gear priority valve operates normally once each flight day.
With engines not operating, pressurize the center hydraulic system using the operational center system pump.
Verify that the main landing gear pressure shown on the landing gear maintenance page is more than 4500 psi.
Turn off the operating center system pump.
Wait at least 10 seconds.
Verify that the main landing gear pressure shown on the landing gear maintenance page is more than 2000 psi.
